title: Sophisticated Australian Nutella Skillet
description: A rich and indulgent dessert featuring Nutella, with a floral and glassy-jelly texture, made in a cast-iron skillet and friendly to those with nut allergies.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, sprinkle the gelatin over 1/4 cup of water and let it sit for 5 minutes to soften.
In a medium saucepan, combine the sugar, 1 cup of water, and lemon juice, and heat over medium heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves.
Bring the sugar mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and add the softened gelatin, stirring until it is fully dissolved.
Add the dried hibiscus flowers to the saucepan and let it steep for 10 minutes, then strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl.
In a large bowl, whip the heavy cream until it forms stiff peaks, then set it aside.
In the cast-iron skillet, melt the butter over medium heat, then add the Nutella and cook, stirring constantly, until it is smooth and warm.
Pour the hibiscus syrup into the skillet and cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture is warm and well combined.
Fold the whipped cream into the Nutella mixture until it is well combined, then pour the mixture into small individual serving cups.
Refrigerate the dessert for at least 2 hours, or until it is set, then serve chilled.
